chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No. 1724 of 2024 (Muneeb Alam Hashmi v. The State) Date Order with signature of Judges For hearing of bail application 21.10.2024 Mr. Mallag Assa Dashti, advocate for the applicant Mr. Muhammad Anwar Mahar, DDPP for the State ----------------------------------- It is alleged that the applicant with the rest of the culprits robbed complainant Syed Arif Riaz of his money, for which, the present case was registered. The applicant having been refused bail by learned IVth-Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi, East, has sought the same from this Court by way of instant bail application under Section 497 Cr.P.C. Heard arguments and perused the record. The name and description of the applicant do not appear in the FIR which even otherwise is lodged with a delay of about one day, which appears to be surprising. The applicant was identified during the identification parade by P.W Samiuddin; his name is not transpired in FIR as a witness, which prima facie suggests that he has been introduced in the investigation by the complainant party at the latter stage. There is no recovery of any sort from the applicant. And more so, complainant Syed Arif Riaz by filing his affidavit has recorded no objection to the release of the applicant on bail. The case has finally been challaned and there is no likelihood of absconsion or tampering with the evidence on the part of the applicant. In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on the point of further inquiry is made. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court.

In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on the point of further inquiry is made. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. Instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E
